note: Ground truth for task extraction. 'must' = a real task any competent PM would create. 'debatable' = reasonable to include or fold into another task; found = fine, missed = not penalized.

tasks.summary: Chase the payment provider about sandbox rejecting test tokens (saved cards blocked)
Finalize and hand off payment error-state designs (card declined / expired / network) in Figma
Implement / wire up the new payment error states in checkout
Get payment decline error copy reviewed by legal (compliance wording)
Fix Android crash on product detail screen when a product has no images (placeholder in carousel)
Write regression tests for the empty-images path and re-run the carousel suite
Raise Android test-device budget (device farm or more phones) with finance
Instrument checkout funnel analytics this sprint, right after the crash fix
Fix search zero-results infinite spinner (show a no-results message)
tasks.why_debatable: Marco said he'd fold it into the error-states work — a separate task or part of gt3 are both defensible.

tasks.summary: Write up the seller photo-nudge idea as product work for a future sprint
tasks.why_debatable: Explicitly 'not this sprint' — a backlog note or a task are both defensible.

tasks.summary: Ship/prepare checkout release without saved cards (saved cards as fast-follow, keep out of release notes)
tasks.why_debatable: This is a decision more than a task; a release-prep task is a reasonable materialization.
